MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON MAR-01-2016 IN 206, DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO CONTIUE TRIAL. ANNA LIU APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. CANDICE HAMANT APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS. AFTER ARGUMENT AND HAVING PARTIES MET AND CONFERRED, THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. THE TRIAL DATE OF 3/14/16 IS CONTINUED TO 5/31/16 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206 PER AGREEMENT OF PARTIES. DISCOVERY PROCEEDINGS ARE NOT RE-OPENED BUT PARTIES AGREE TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO THE SPECIFICS OF DISCOVERY ISSUES. PARTIES AGREE TO COMPLETE MEDIATION NO LATER THAN 4/20/16. EXPERT DISCOVERY AND CUTOFFS WILL TRACK THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING. JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART; CLERK: R. VELUZ; COURT REPORTER: ANGELA POURTABIB, CSR#13714; (415-601-7054). (206)

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-01-2016 Text Ruling

Defendant'S Motion For Protective Order Re: Depositions And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ANNA LIU, ESQ. of Steven Adair MacDonald Partners, P.C., Tel No. 415-956-6488 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. GREGORY DE LA PENA, ESQ. of de la Pena & Holiday, Tel No. 415-268-8004 appearing for the Kwan defendants and Defendant Reliant Corporation.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants' motion for a protective order is denied and all requests for sanctions are denied. The request for a protective order is now moot since defendants' motion to disqualify the MacDonald firm will be heard and decided at the same time as this motion. Moreover, even if the motion were not moot, it would be denied on its merits since The requests for sanctions are denied because defendants had substantial justification for filing the motion, particularly given the MacDonald firm's refusal to comply with the legitimate request to provide all documents in its possession relating to the earlier matter. The Court adds to its tentative ruling that Defendant Nicholas Kwan shall appear for his deposition at a mutually convenient time. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Counsel - (Especially Set On Order Shortening Time)
Off Calendar Feb-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ANNA LIU, ESQ. of Steven Adair MacDonald Partners, P.C., Tel No. 415-956-6488 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. GREGORY DE LA PENA, ESQ. of de la Pena & Holiday, Tel No. 415-268-8004 appearing for the Kwan defendants and Defendant Reliant Corporation.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS MARCELLA KWAN, NICHOLAS KWAN, AND ROGER C.P. KWAN'S Motion To Disqualify Counsel. Defendants' motion to disqualify the MacDonald firm is denied. Defendants have not satisfied their burden that there was a substantial relationship between the earlier matter where defendants, or some of them, were represented by Mr. MacDonald the current case. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
